overexcitement.description: I had a strong intuition that path-indexed-with-shared-nodes would distinguish from MemPalace's node-indexed hierarchy. The intuition was reasonable but MemPalace + A-Mem + Memex(RL) collectively cover most of the design space, leaving a narrow refinement claim rather than a paradigm shift.

honest_note: MemPalace 2604.21284 is genuinely strong substantive prior art. It explicitly claims to be 'the first systematic application of spatial memory metaphors as an organizing principle for AI memory systems' and went viral April 2026 (47k GitHub stars). The Aboriginal-songlines specialization adds path-conditioned-multi-interpretation, which IS a distinguishing feature not in MemPalace's Wings/Rooms/Drawers hierarchy. So under a strict 'is the exact mechanism published' reading, the candidate might be more novel than the verdict suggests. Under the saturation_evidence broader reading ('is the umbrella analogy taken'), it's clearly FAIL. I'm marking FAIL per the broader reading; logging the impulse to argue narrower-novelty here honestly. The Memex(RL) 'Indexed Experience Memory' is a closer architectural cousin than my candidate framing acknowledges.
